title: NIS2 Art.28-30 domain registration, DNS integrity and information-sharing evidence

dataSources: `index=dns` `sourcetype=dns:query` or `sourcetype=infoblox:dns` with `query`, `answer`, `response_code`, `src_ip` from Splunk Add-on for Infoblox ([Splunkbase 2934](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2934)); CIM **Network_Resolution** data model fields `dest`, `src`, `answer`; `lookup=domain_registry.csv` for approved nameservers and registrar IDs; `index=threat_intel` `sourcetype=stix`; optional `index=grc` `sourcetype=grc:sharing_arrangement`.
app: Splunk Add-on for Infoblox ([Splunkbase 2934](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2934)); Splunk CIM ([Splunkbase 1621](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)); Splunk Enterprise Security ([Splunkbase 263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)) for intel workflow.
spl:
| inputlookup domain_registry.csv
| rename fqdn as query
| join query type=left [
    search index=dns (sourcetype=dns:query OR sourcetype=infoblox:dns) earliest=-24h
    | stats dc(answer) as answer_cardinality values(answer) as last_answers by query
]
| eval max_ans=coalesce(max_answers_allowed,5)
| eval ns_drift=if(isnotnull(answer_cardinality) AND answer_cardinality>max_ans,1,0)
| append [
    search index=threat_intel sourcetype=stix earliest=-7d threat_type=domain
    | stats dc(value) as observed_domains by feed_name
    | where observed_domains<1
]
| lookup nis2_dns_kfp_exceptions query OUTPUT exception_until
| where ns_drift=1 OR (isnotnull(feed_name) AND observed_domains<1)
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table query, registrar_tag, answer_cardinality, last_answers, feed_name, observed_domains, ns_drift
description: Correlates the approved domain registry lookup against live DNS answer diversity per fully qualified name and adds a lightweight STIX domain-feed health check so registrar data, resolution integrity, and cooperation telemetry stay aligned for digital infrastructure teams.
value: Brand-protection and public-trust leaders can show regulators that name-resolution anomalies and stale information-sharing feeds are operationally monitored alongside the legal register, narrowing exposure during coordinated takedowns.
implementation: (1) Deploy Splunkbase 2934 for Infoblox or map BIND logs to `dns:query`. (2) Populate `domain_registry.csv`. (3) Inhibit alert noise with `nis2_dns_kfp_exceptions`. (4) Integrate STIX feeds via ES. (5) Archive weekly PDF to `index=audit_evidence`.
visualization: DNS answer cardinality heatmap, table of `query` vs expected nameservers, intel feed single values.

references.title: Splunk Common Information Model — Network Resolution

knownFalsePositives:
• **GeoDNS or load-balancing pools** — Distinguish: many legitimate `answer` values during rotation. Suppress: maintain `max_answers_allowed` column in `domain_registry.csv` and compare with `where answer_cardinality>max_answers_allowed`.
• **Split-horizon internal views** — Distinguish: queries from `corp` resolvers differ. Suppress: filter `src_ip` with `cidrmatch` to exclude internal recursion.
• **Intel feed maintenance blackout** — Distinguish: vendor publishes schedule. Suppress: time-bound exception register referencing vendor ticket.
• **Wildcard marketing campaign DNS** — Distinguish: CNAME chains to third-party CDNs. Suppress: lookup `nis2_dns_kfp_exceptions` keyed by campaign id for 30 days.
exclusions: Does not administer registrar portals, rotate DNSSEC keys, or attest contractual relationships with ISPs; cooperation evidence is technical telemetry only unless legal adopts the feed.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.55`, supporting `dns:query` / `infoblox:dns` slices, STIX feed statistics, registry lookup versions, and tamper-evident export in `index=audit_evidence`.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Point a lab resolver at a test zone that returns five unexpected additional `answer` values for a production `fqdn` listed in `domain_registry.csv` with a low `max_answers_allowed`, then verify `ns_drift=1` appears in the panel within the 24-hour window.
controlTest.negativeScenario: When answers stay within the registry’s allowed cardinality and the STIX domain feed reports at least one fresh `value` per `feed_name` in the last week, the alert stays quiet across a replay test harness that excludes deliberate fault injection.

cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Resolution where nodename=Network_Resolution.DNS by Network_Resolution.query span=1h
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Infoblox Grid or open-source DNS logging capable of emitting query/answer pairs with millisecond timestamps; forwarder tier with 15-minute install of Splunk Add-on for Infoblox ([Splunkbase 2934](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2934)).
• Splunk CIM 5.x+ with Network_Resolution dataset accelerated for 30 days (`Settings` > `Data models`).
• Lookup `domain_registry.csv` with columns `fqdn`, `registrar_tag`, `max_answers_allowed`, `expected_ns`.
• Enterprise Security optional for TAXII domain collections landing in `index=threat_intel` `sourcetype=stix`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
On Infoblox Data Connector or syslog path, confirm events land in `index=dns` `sourcetype=infoblox:dns`. For BIND, use `dns:query` and `TRANSFORMS` to extract `query`, `answer`. Retention: 90 days hot, longer frozen for incident replay. Document peak EPS (often 50k+/s for large ISPs) — apply `queue` tuning in `outputs.conf` on forwarders.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Schedule hourly; use summary indexing if cardinality high.

```spl
| inputlookup domain_registry.csv
| rename fqdn as query
| eval max_ans=coalesce(max_answers_allowed,5)
| join query type=left [
    search index=dns sourcetype IN ("dns:query","infoblox:dns") earliest=-24h
    | stats dc(answer) as answer_cardinality values(answer) as last_answers by query
]
| eval ns_drift=if(isnotnull(answer_cardinality) AND answer_cardinality>max_ans,1,0)
```

Append the domain-sharing subsearch, exception lookup, and filters exactly as in the saved search `spl` field.

Step 3 — Validate
Choose a stable marketing hostname; run `dig` from outside and inside; compare Splunk `answer_cardinality` with expected Anycast responses. Validate false-positive suppression using a dev subdomain with intentional GeoDNS.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: row 1 single value when intel feeds report zero new domain indicators; row 2 DNS drift table; row 3 map of resolver `src_ip`. Route alerts to DNS engineering Slack with runbook link to registrar portal.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No answer field** — props missing FIELDALGS; reload `splunkd` after updating `props.conf`.
• **Join timeout** — reduce window to 6h or pre-aggregate with `tstats` from CIM.
• **False zero-count intel downloads** — feed paused; check Enterprise Security download logs.
• **Permission errors** — role lacks `threat_intel`; update `authorize.conf`.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the internet’s address book for your public names and compare what the world sees with what you officially expect. We also keep an eye on automated feeds of bad-domain tips so a silent stop in those feeds does not go unnoticed.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate the Network_Resolution data model (summary range 30 days) before relying on `tstats` variants during peak query volume.
